title: IT managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as electronic data processing, information systems, systems analysis, and computer programming. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. Plan, implement, upgrade, or monitor security measures for the protection of computer networks and information. Assess system vulnerabilities for security risks and propose and implement risk mitigation strategies. May ensure appropriate security controls are in place that will safeguard digital files and vital electronic infrastructure. May respond to computer security breaches and viruses. (Based on 22 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Manage backup, security and user help systems for computer networks.
Confer with the board of directors to determine budget requirements and plan overall operations.
Recruit, hire, train and supervise staff or participate in staffing decisions.
Develop computer information resources, ensuring data security and control, along with disaster recovery plans.
Evaluate the organization's technology use and needs, recommending improvements such as hardware and software upgrades.
Train users and promote security awareness to ensure system security and improve server and network efficiency.
Direct daily operations of the department, analyzing workflow, establishing priorities, and setting deadlines.
Monitor use of data files and regulate access to safeguard information in computer files.
Perform risk assessments and execute tests of data processing systems to ensure functionality and security measures.
Design, configure, and test computer hardware, networking software and operating system software for effective network operations.
Conduct software compatibility tests and monitor program performance to ensure efficient operations.
Identify program deviance from standards and suggest modifications to maintain compliance and improve performance.
Document technical factors such as server load, bandwidth, database performance, and browser compatibility for system improvements.
Provide users with technical support and troubleshoot computer-related problems as they arise.
Create and maintain accessible, retrievable computer archives and databases, utilizing current advances in storage technology.
Develop specialized computer software routines or business applications to customize geographic information or integrate different products.
Create visual representations of geospatial data using analytical modeling or three-dimensional renderings.
Provide technical support to users or clients regarding the maintenance or operation of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases.
Analyze and configure applications by conferring with users to troubleshoot and address their needs.
Train staff on information security standards, policies, and best practices, and provide technical support.
Conduct investigations of information security breaches to identify vulnerabilities and assess damage.
Coordinate assessments of information security systems, including vulnerability assessments and monitoring for intrusions.
Develop or implement software tools to assist in the detection and analysis of security threats.
Review security assessments to ensure compliance with cybersecurity standards and regulations.
Test programs or databases, correct errors, and assist in the development of testing routines and procedures.
Gather cyber intelligence to identify vulnerabilities and assess the physical security of servers or network devices.
Maintain knowledge of laws and regulations regarding digital media handling, and update corporate policies for cyber security.
Design and implement security solutions to address known vulnerabilities in systems and applications.
Assess industry trends and evaluate new technologies relevant to database systems or cybersecurity.
Initiate or approve modifications to project plans and ensure project timelines are adhered to.
Perform user testing or usage analyses to determine Web sites' effectiveness or usability.
Provide training or technical assistance in Web site implementation or use and inform users of application changes and updates.
Develop or implement procedures for ongoing Web site revision and document application changes or procedures.
Test issues such as system integration, performance, and system security on a regular schedule or after major modifications.
Track, compile, and analyze Web site usage data and develop metrics for performance evaluation.
Identify and communicate levels of access and security, and implement security measures for Web sites, such as firewalls or encryption.
Identify, recommend, and evaluate hardware or software technologies to achieve desired performance and configure servers to specifications.
Develop or approve project plans, schedules, and budgets, and monitor project milestones and deliverables.
Assess current or future customer needs by communicating directly with customers and conducting surveys.
Provide technical guidance or support for system development or troubleshooting, and monitor system operations for problems.
Develop and implement data extraction procedures from various systems, and collaborate with teams to select appropriate design solutions.
Conduct needs assessments to identify information system requirements and assist in deployment of new management systems.
Manage project execution to ensure adherence to budget, schedule, and scope, and assign duties and responsibilities to project personnel.
Collaborate with system architects and design analysts to understand business or industry requirements, and create technical documentation.
Implement updates, upgrades, and patches in a timely manner to maintain system integrity and effectiveness.
Perform risk assessments to develop response strategies for potential security breaches or system vulnerabilities.
Monitor systems for intrusions or denial of service attacks and report security breaches to appropriate personnel.
Investigate system component suitability for specified purposes and make recommendations to enhance overall performance.
Direct the analysis, development, and operation of complete computer systems to meet organizational needs.
Communicate project information through presentations, technical reports, or white papers to keep stakeholders informed.
Confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems, and monitor project milestones and deliverables.
Create project status presentations for delivery to customers or project personnel, and report project status to managers.
Develop or update project plans including objectives, technologies, schedules, funding, and staffing, and propose modifications to project plans as necessary.
Identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing project objectives and schedules, and recruit or hire project personnel.
Monitor costs incurred by project staff to identify budget issues and prepare budget estimates, progress reports, or cost tracking reports.
Monitor the performance of project team members to provide feedback and ensure adherence to quality standards in project deliverables.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials and schedule or facilitate project meetings.
Request and review project updates to ensure deadlines are met and plan, schedule, or coordinate project activities to meet deadlines.
Produce and distribute project documents and submit project deliverables to clients.
